items.final_wording: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

items.rationale: The exact history and examination section supports recording focused examination and general appearance only when consented to and performed; it does not mandate a sexual-assault examination.

items.final_wording: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

source.title: Management of Domestic Abuse
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
source.population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
source.setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
source.exclusions: Exact only for confidential, clinician-led enquiry where suspicion exists, documentation at disclosure, injury documentation and recording child-welfare concerns. It is not used for universal screening or automatic police, shelter or referral action.

exact_location.heading: Disclosure, injury records, child welfare and support

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

items.rationale: The exact RCEM disclosure section supports recording safeguarding concern, specialist discussion and handover or follow-up only when disclosure occurred and actions were completed.

items.final_wording: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

added_items.final_wording: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

added_items.rationale: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports documenting the person’s concerns or goals, associated needs, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed safety context only when discussed; it does not assert violence or mandate action.

added_items.final_wording: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook

source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

added_items.rationale: The exact WHO documentation section supports a consent-based record of complaints, symptoms, signs or injuries, completed risk discussion and clinician-entered plan only when actually discussed.

added_items.final_wording: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

exact_location.heading: History and physical examination after sexual violence

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

added_items.rationale: The exact history and examination section supports recording focused examination and general appearance only when consented to and performed; it does not mandate a sexual-assault examination.

added_items.final_wording: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

source.title: Management of Domestic Abuse
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
source.population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
source.setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
source.exclusions: Exact only for confidential, clinician-led enquiry where suspicion exists, documentation at disclosure, injury documentation and recording child-welfare concerns. It is not used for universal screening or automatic police, shelter or referral action.

exact_location.heading: Disclosure, injury records, child welfare and support

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

added_items.rationale: The exact RCEM disclosure section supports recording safeguarding concern, specialist discussion and handover or follow-up only when disclosure occurred and actions were completed.

added_items.final_wording: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.
